summary: Small external Dolphin SDK header fix to make decomp.py/m2ctx processing tolerate CodeWarrior-specific constructs. The PR guards Metrowerks absolute-address variable declarations behind `defined(__MWERKS__) && !defined(M2CTX)` and avoids `#undef` directives in GXVert.h when M2CTX is active, because the context generator apparently does not support them.
what_changed: In `GXVert.h`, changed the `GXWGFifo : GXFIFO_ADDR` absolute-address declaration guard from `#ifdef __MWERKS__` to `#if defined(__MWERKS__) && !defined(M2CTX)`, preserving the pointer macro fallback for non-Metrowerks or M2CTX parsing.

In `GXVert.h`, wrapped the cleanup `#undef FUNC_1PARAM`, `FUNC_2PARAM`, `FUNC_3PARAM`, `FUNC_4PARAM`, `FUNC_INDEX8`, and `FUNC_INDEX16` block in `#if !defined(M2CTX)` with the comment `// undef not supported`.
In `hw_regs.h`, changed the absolute-address hardware register declarations such as `__VIRegs`, `__PIRegs`, and `__MEMRegs` to be used only when `__MWERKS__` is defined and `M2CTX` is not defined.
In `os.h`, changed the absolute-address OS globals such as `__OSPhysicalMemSize`, `__OSTVMode`, and `__gUnkThread1` to be used only when `__MWERKS__` is defined and `M2CTX` is not defined.
smart_moves: Used the existing `M2CTX` preprocessor flag to isolate constructs that are valid for the Metrowerks compiler but problematic for context generation, instead of removing the SDK-style declarations globally.
Kept runtime/compiler-facing behavior intact for normal `__MWERKS__` builds while giving decomp.py/m2ctx a parseable fallback path.
Localized the fix to external Dolphin headers and changed only the preprocessor conditions needed for tooling compatibility.
Documented the unusual macro cleanup guard with a direct comment: `// undef not supported`.
decomp_lessons: External SDK headers may contain Metrowerks-specific syntax, especially absolute-address declarations using `symbol : address`, that decomp context tooling cannot parse even though the original compiler accepts it.
When adapting headers for decomp.py or m2ctx, prefer gating unsupported syntax with `&& !defined(M2CTX)` rather than weakening the header for all builds.

If m2ctx cannot handle a preprocessor feature such as `#undef`, guard that feature explicitly under `#if !defined(M2CTX)` so macro definitions remain available or at least do not break context generation.
For hardware register and memory-mapped global declarations, provide a parser-friendly fallback path while preserving exact CodeWarrior declarations for real `__MWERKS__` compilation.
naming_conventions: The PR uses `M2CTX` as the feature/test macro for context-generation-specific header behavior.
Metrowerks-specific branches continue to use `__MWERKS__`; compatibility guards are written as `#if defined(__MWERKS__) && !defined(M2CTX)` rather than plain `#ifdef __MWERKS__`.
SDK register/global names remain unchanged, including `GXWGFifo`, `__VIRegs`, `__PIRegs`, `__MEMRegs`, `__OSPhysicalMemSize`, `__OSTVMode`, and `__gUnkThread1`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching changes are shown. The relevance is indirect: making external Dolphin headers parseable for decomp.py/m2ctx supports generating accurate decompilation contexts without changing source semantics.

For CodeWarrior absolute-address declarations, keep them active for compiler-accurate paths but disable them for m2ctx so decomp tooling can use pointer-macro or non-absolute-declaration alternatives.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale visible in the diff is the inline comment in `GXVert.h`: `// undef not supported`.
follow_up_queries: Find other Dolphin or SDK headers using `#ifdef __MWERKS__` with absolute-address declarations that may also need `&& !defined(M2CTX)` guards.
Search for remaining `#undef` directives in headers consumed by m2ctx/decomp.py to confirm whether they need similar `#if !defined(M2CTX)` protection.
Check whether decomp.py context generation relies on `M2CTX` consistently across all extern headers.
